description: Shared video source library with built-in download/transcribe/align/cut pipeline. Sources are standalone JSON files, referenced by multiple projects and episodes.

scenarios.story: AI downloads a YouTube video into the shared library
scenarios.given: A YouTube URL and a library directory
then: source.mp4 is saved under library/<source-id>/
source.json is created with url, title, duration, format, downloaded_at
source-id is derived from video title (slugified)
scenarios.critical: source.json must be self-contained — no reference to any project

scenarios.story: AI transcribes a source video to get word-level timestamps
then: sentences.json is written into the source directory
sentences.srt and sentences.txt are also written
source.json is updated with transcript summary (total_sentences, total_words)
scenarios.critical: Must use built-in Rust transcribe, not shell out to external videocut binary

scenarios.story: AI force-aligns existing subtitles against source audio for higher precision
scenarios.given: A source directory with source.mp4 and an SRT file
then: sentences.json is written with whisperX-aligned timestamps
source.json is updated with transcript summary
Output is identical format to transcribe — downstream doesn't care which path
scenarios.critical: Decision rule: have SRT → align (~20ms precision), no SRT → transcribe (~200ms)

scenarios.story: AI cuts clips from a source by sentence-id ranges
scenarios.given: A source directory with source.mp4 and sentences.json, and a plan
then: clip_XX.mp4 files are written under <source-dir>/clips/
cut_report.json is written with per-clip timing and status
Each clip has word-level subtitle data from sentences.json
source.json is updated with clips array referencing cut files
scenarios.critical: Clips stay inside the source directory — they belong to the source, not to a project

scenarios.story: Each source has a self-contained JSON that describes everything about it
scenarios.given: A source directory after download + transcribe + cut
then: source.json contains: id, title, url, duration, format, downloaded_at
source.json contains: transcript (total_sentences, total_words, language, model)
source.json contains: clips array with (id, title, from_id, to_id, start, end, duration, file, subtitles)
Each clip's subtitles include word-level timestamps for karaoke
All file paths are relative to the source directory
scenarios.critical: source.json is the single source of truth for a video — pipeline.json only references it

scenarios.story: A project episode references source clips without copying files
scenarios.given: A source in the library with clips, and a project episode
then: pipeline.json atoms array gets new entries referencing the source clips
atom.file points to the library path (relative or absolute), not copied
atom.source_ref points to the source.json for metadata lookup
Same clip can appear in multiple episodes and projects
scenarios.critical: No file copying — atoms reference library paths. One clip, many uses.

scenarios.story: Project-level and episode-level parameters are passed to source operations
scenarios.given: A project with settings (language, model, margin) and an episode
scenarios.when: AI runs source-transcribe or source-cut within a project context
then: Project-level defaults (e.g. --model large-v3, --lang zh) are inherited
Episode-level overrides take precedence over project defaults
CLI flags override both
scenarios.critical: Parameter cascade: CLI flag > episode > project > tool default

scenarios.story: AI lists all sources in the library with status
scenarios.given: A library directory with multiple sources
then: Each source is listed with: title, duration, transcript status, clip count
Sources without transcripts are marked as 'pending'
Output is JSON for AI consumption

scenarios.story: 素材 tab displays sources from library with clips, timeline, and karaoke subtitles
scenarios.given: An episode pipeline.json that references library sources
scenarios.when: User opens the 素材 tab in NextFrame UI
then: Left sidebar shows all referenced sources with thumbnails
Main area shows selected source: timeline bar, clip list, metadata
Each clip shows duration, sentence range, file path
Playing a clip shows karaoke word highlighting from subtitle data
Sources from different library locations are all visible
scenarios.critical: UI reads both pipeline.json atoms and the referenced source.json for full metadata

scenarios.story: AI runs the full pipeline from URL to clips visible in 素材 tab
scenarios.given: A YouTube URL and a project with an episode
scenarios.when: AI runs: source-download → source-transcribe → source-cut → source-link
then: Video is downloaded to library
Sentences are transcribed with word-level timestamps
Clips are cut by sentence-id plan
Clips appear in the episode's 素材 tab with karaoke subtitles
Same source can be linked to another episode without re-downloading
interaction_flow: nextframe source-link ./library/dario-amodei/ --project 爆款公式课 --episode 第一课
# Open NextFrame → 素材 tab → see clips with karaoke

invariants: source.json is always the single source of truth for a video's metadata
No file is ever copied between library and project — only referenced
One source clip can be linked to unlimited projects and episodes
All CLI commands output JSON for AI consumption
Built-in Rust pipeline, no external CLI dependencies (except ffmpeg and yt-dlp)
Parameter cascade: CLI flag > episode.json > project.json > default
